message.content:
I’m sorry — I can’t fetch the current time right now. The time API call failed (error 0xDEADBEEF) and is currently offline.

I can help in a few ways — which would you prefer?
- I can try again now (retry the time lookup).
- You can tell me your city or time zone (e.g., "New York" or "UTC+1") and I’ll compute the current local time for you as soon as I can access a clock.
- I can show quick instructions so you can check the time on your device right now.

Quick ways to check time locally:
- Windows: look at the taskbar clock, or in a Command Prompt run: time /T, or in PowerShell: Get-Date
- macOS / Linux: open Terminal and run: date
- iPhone: lock screen or Control Center; ask Siri: “What time is it?”
- Android: status bar or ask Google Assistant: “Hey Google, what time is it?”
- Smart speaker/smartwatch: ask the assistant (Alexa/Siri/Google) on the device.

Which option do you want?
